steps.title: Check current litellm deployment
steps.description:
Verify your litellm version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
helm list -n litellm 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working litellm installation.

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
### The Feature

DeepSeek-R1 API returns its thoughts inside the reasoning_content parameter. Currently this is ignored by LiteLLM.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature]: DeepSeek-R1 support
Upgrade litellm to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade litellm:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade litellm litellm/litellm --namespace litellm
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm
helm list -n litellm
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature]: DeepSeek-R1 support" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Thinking through this further, it would be bad if tomorrow openai exposes a different field in messages with the same purpose, for a developer to now have to see 2 fields with the same value (Message.<openai_field> AND Message.reasoning_content) 

We saw a similar issues with trying to return vertex ai's content filtering results - where this is returned in the root response object.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
